summary: All 31 provided scenarios for the User Registration function are fully covered by 41 test cases. Test cases are traceable to requirement IDs, clear, executable, and verifiable. Coverage includes positive, negative, validation, boundary, business rule, security, UX/UI, and integration categories as per the test scope. Previous coverage issues have been resolved. No missing scenarios or weak test cases identified. The test cases are ready for QA execution.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review noted that integration scenarios for email verification system and token expiration/resend logic were not explicitly covered. Additional test cases (TC032-TC041) have been added to cover these integration and business rule scenarios.
resolved_issues.resolution: Added 10 integration and business rule test cases covering email verification system dependency, token expiration (24 hours), resend cooldown (60 seconds), boundary timing for resend, and rate limiting on resend.

final_recommendations.description: TC006 (Register with password length exactly 12 characters) uses test data 'Abc123@#xyz' which is 12 characters and meets complexity rules. However, the test steps are generic and do not specify the exact values. Update test steps to include the exact password value for clarity and repeatability.

TC021 (Register with password containing single spaces) uses test data 'Pass1@ word' which contains a single space. The expected results state that registration should succeed. However, the requirement does not explicitly state that single spaces are allowed; only consecutive spaces are forbidden. Confirm with the product owner that single spaces are permitted in passwords. If not, update the test case to be negative.

TC033 and TC040 both test the 24-hour expiration boundary but use different expected results for the same time offset (24 hours and 1 minute). TC033 expects an error message, TC040 expects the same. This is consistent, but ensure the test environment can reliably simulate 24-hour time jumps. Consider using a mock or time manipulation tool to avoid long wait times.
